overrides.override_summary: Cure periods modified: 5-day for payments, 60-day for other defaults, 75-day maximum. Section 16.3 entirely rewritten.
overrides.why_it_matters: Longer cure periods give CA franchisees more time to fix problems before termination

overrides.override_summary: Market Withdrawal provision: franchisor can withdraw from state/area with no liability, but cannot prevent franchisee from retaining business control
overrides.why_it_matters: If Panda exits your market, you keep the business but lose the brand

overrides.override_summary: Post-termination repurchase of items at price paid minus depreciation (MACRS or IRS schedule, whichever lower)
overrides.why_it_matters: CA law requires fair compensation — depreciation formula defined

overrides.override_summary: Corp Code Section 31512 voids waivers of rights under Franchise Investment Law; B&P Code 20010 voids waivers under Franchise Relations Act
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees cannot waive statutory franchise protections even if release is signed

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration and litigation in Rosemead, California. Prospective franchisees encouraged to consult counsel re: venue restrictions.
overrides.why_it_matters: Arbitration is in-state for CA franchisees but out-of-state for all others

overrides.override_summary: All arbitration/litigation must be conducted in Kansas under Kansas law
overrides.why_it_matters: Kansas franchisees get in-state dispute resolution — a significant override of the default CA venue

overrides.override_summary: Kansas law governs every franchise agreement performed in Kansas
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides default California choice of law

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require indemnification for promisee's own negligence or intentional acts
overrides.why_it_matters: Limits franchisor's ability to shift blame for its own mistakes to franchisee

overrides.override_summary: Section 22 entirely rewritten — NASAA standard; cannot waive franchise law claims or disclaim reliance on FDD statements
overrides.why_it_matters: Strongest integration clause override — preserves franchisee's right to rely on FDD representations

overrides.override_summary: Good cause required for termination; 30-day cure opportunity; fair compensation on non-renewal (repurchase at FMV)
overrides.why_it_matters: MI provides strong termination and non-renewal protections beyond the license agreement

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration or litigation outside Michigan is void
overrides.why_it_matters: MI franchisees cannot be forced to arbitrate in California

overrides.override_summary: Cannot refuse transfer without good cause; ROFR preserved but standards clarified
Additional risk factors required on cover page; franchisor cannot negotiate less favorable terms than FDD
overrides.why_it_matters: NY provides disclosure-level protections and prevents bait-and-switch

overrides.override_summary: Cannot cancel franchise without reasonable cause per Section 13.1-564 of Virginia Retail Franchising Act
overrides.why_it_matters: Any termination without reasonable cause is unlawful in Virginia

overrides.override_summary: NASAA Required Statement: No waiver of claims under state franchise law; no disclaiming reliance on franchisor/franchise seller statements
overrides.why_it_matters: Baseline anti-waiver protection in all 14+ registration states
